chooser.title: How would you like to start?

fromScratch.title: Start from scratch
fromScratch.description: Empty workspace. Useful when you already know which rules you want to model.

template.title: Use a template
template.description: Start from a ready-made example (engagement, reward on completion, speed bonus...).

extend.title: Extend an existing strategy
extend.description: Wrap a built-in (e.g. default) with pre/post rules without rewriting it.

simulate.title: Test strategy
simulate.mockHint: Override whitelisted fields with fixed values, e.g. {"task.measurements_count": 1}.

parentSchema.title: Parent: {{name}}
parentSchema.name: Name
parentSchema.noVariables: This strategy exposes no editable variables.
parentSchema.hint: Use the "Parent overrides" toolbox category to tweak these values.

alerts.noParent: Select a parent strategy before saving.
alerts.noParentSim: Select a parent strategy before testing.
alerts.notAStrategyBundle: The file does not look like a strategy bundle: missing astJson.
alerts.unknownError: Unknown error when contacting the backend.

steps.welcome: Welcome to the strategy editor. Here you can design point rules without writing code.
steps.name: Give your strategy a clear name — you'll see it when assigning it to a game or task.
steps.mode: Pick whether you start from scratch or extend an existing strategy.
steps.workspace: Drag blocks from the side panel onto this canvas. Snap them together like puzzle pieces to form rules.
steps.simulate: When you have a rule, test it here with a fictitious user and task — nothing is persisted to production.
steps.save: Save as draft as often as you like. It only reaches production when an admin publishes the version.
steps.history: Every save creates a version. From here you can view history and roll back.
